conversation.content: build a car game , i need able to drive the car using ASDW keys in map with some road and building and stuff

steps.description: RoadSystem_Agent will create a multi-lane road system within the game. The agent will use Instance.new to create Parts for the road segments, ensuring realistic dimensions (200 studs long, 24 studs wide for a 2-lane road). The road will be positioned above the Baseplate, with Y-coordinate set to 0.1 studs. The agent will also create sidewalks adjacent to the road using Parts, ensuring proper dimensions (200 studs long, 6 studs wide). The road and sidewalks will be colored appropriately (dark gray for the road, light brown for the sidewalks) and given suitable materials (Asphalt for the road, Concrete for the sidewalks). All road and sidewalk Parts will be set to Anchored = true.

BuildingSystem_Agent will create several buildings around the road system. The agent will use Instance.new to create Parts for the building structures, ensuring realistic dimensions (30-80 studs tall, 30-50 studs wide and deep for small to large buildings). The buildings will be positioned at least 40-60 studs apart from each other and from the road. The agent will also create windows and doors using Parts, ensuring proper dimensions (4x7 studs for doors, 4x4 studs for windows). The buildings will be colored and given materials to resemble real-world structures (Medium stone grey for the main structure, Glass for windows). All building Parts will be set to Anchored = true.

CarSystem_Agent will insert a drivable car into the game using the insert_free_model command to fetch a suitable car model from the Toolbox. The agent will follow the 4-turn workflow: TURN 1: Use insert_free_model to fetch the car, then use fetch_explorer_state. END TURN. TURN 2: Configure the car for driving by setting Anchored = false on all car parts and adjusting the VehicleSeat properties (MaxSpeed, Torque) if it exists. END TURN. TURN 3: Add a ProximityPrompt to the car for player interaction and a Script inside the car model to handle driving entry. END TURN. TURN 4: Verify and complete.

EnvironmentSystem_Agent will enhance the game environment by adding trees, street lamps, and other decorative elements. The agent will use Instance.new to create Parts for these elements, ensuring realistic dimensions (2-4 studs wide, 12 studs tall for tree trunks, 12 studs diameter for tree canopies, 1 stud wide, 16 studs tall for street lamp posts). The agent will position these elements around the road and buildings, ensuring proper spacing and visual variety. All environmental Parts will be set to Anchored = true.

SpawnSystem_Agent will create a SpawnLocation for players to start the game. The agent will use Instance.new to create a Part for the SpawnLocation, ensuring proper dimensions (8x1x8 studs). The SpawnLocation will be positioned away from any hazards (cars, buildings) and set to Anchored = true.
